title: The Rise of AI Influencers: Are You Being Fooled Online?
subtitle: Companies are increasingly using computer-generated people to sell products, leading to calls for better rules and honesty.

summary.en: Brands are now using AI-generated people to promote products on social media platforms. These digital characters often look like real humans, making it hard for customers to tell the difference. Experts worry that this lack of transparency could lead to people being misled by advertisements. While some regions are creating new laws to require clear labeling of AI content, other areas still lack these protections. Consumers are encouraged to be more careful about what they see online as the technology becomes more common.
summary.zh: 品牌目前正利用人工智慧生成的人物在社群媒體平台上推廣產品。這些數位角色通常看起來與真人無異，導致顧客難以分辨。專家擔心這種缺乏透明度的做法可能導致民眾被廣告誤導。雖然部分地區正在制定新法，要求清楚標示人工智慧內容，但其他地區仍缺乏此類保護措施。隨著這項技術日益普及，建議消費者對網路上所見的內容應更加謹慎。

paragraphs.en: When you scroll through your social media feed, you might see a person recommending a new app, a fashion brand, or a travel service. They look like real people sharing their honest opinions. However, a new investigation by The Guardian has revealed that many of these influencers are not real humans at all. Instead, they are computer-generated images created by artificial intelligence (AI).
paragraphs.zh: 當您瀏覽社群媒體動態時，可能會看到有人推薦一款新應用程式、一個時尚品牌或一項旅遊服務。他們看起來就像真實的人在分享誠懇的意見。然而，衛報 (The Guardian) 的一項最新調查揭露，這些網紅中有許多根本不是真人，而是由人工智慧 (AI) 所創造的電腦生成影像。

paragraphs.en: Companies are quietly using these digital influencers to promote their products. The goal is often to make the advertisements feel like genuine customer experiences. Because these AI characters look so realistic, most people cannot tell that they are looking at a machine-made image rather than a real person. In some cases, the people who create this content are asked to sign non-disclosure agreements—legal contracts that prevent them from talking about their work—which keeps the process hidden from the public.
paragraphs.zh: 企業正悄悄利用這些數位網紅來推廣產品。其目標通常是讓廣告看起來像是真實的顧客體驗。由於這些人工智慧角色看起來非常逼真，大多數人無法分辨自己看到的是機器生成的影像，而非真人。在某些情況下，製作這些內容的人員會被要求簽署保密協議（即禁止他們談論工作內容的法律契約），這使得整個過程對大眾隱而不宣。

paragraphs.en: There are several examples of this practice appearing on popular platforms like Instagram. For instance, an app called Once, which helps people take photos at events, has been linked to potential AI-generated influencer content. In one video, a woman appears to be a bride talking about how much she liked using the app at her wedding. When asked about this, the company did not provide a comment. Another brand, a housing design app called Maket, admitted to using AI influencers. The company stated that they use these digital characters to test new marketing ideas on a small scale. They described the practice as an experiment to see what kind of content catches the attention of their audience.
paragraphs.zh: 在 Instagram 等熱門平台上，這類做法已有不少案例。例如，一款名為 Once 的應用程式，其功能是協助人們在活動中拍照，該程式已被連結到潛在的人工智慧生成網紅內容。在一段影片中，一名女子看似新娘，談論著她在婚禮上使用該應用程式的喜愛程度。當被問及此事時，該公司並未提供評論。另一個品牌，名為 Maket 的居家設計應用程式，則承認使用了人工智慧網紅。該公司表示，他們利用這些數位角色在小範圍內測試新的行銷點子。他們將此做法描述為一項實驗，旨在觀察何種內容能吸引受眾的注意。

paragraphs.en: Sometimes, the technology is not perfect, which can lead to strange mistakes. A fashion brand called Ashle once posted a photo of a woman wearing their clothes at a restaurant. Sharp-eyed viewers noticed that the woman in the picture appeared to have an extra finger—a common error in AI-generated images. After being contacted by The Guardian, the brand deleted the photos from their social media page. A spokesperson for Ashle explained that while their clothes are real and handmade, they had used AI for some of their early marketing images. They claimed the photos were removed because those designs were no longer for sale, not because of the AI technology itself.
paragraphs.zh: 有時，這項技術並不完美，可能會導致奇怪的錯誤。一個名為 Ashle 的時尚品牌曾發布一張女子在餐廳穿著他們服飾的照片。眼尖的觀眾發現照片中的女子似乎多了一根手指，這是人工智慧生成影像中常見的錯誤。在接獲衛報聯繫後，該品牌將照片從社群媒體頁面上刪除。Ashle 的發言人解釋說，雖然他們的服飾是真實且手工製作的，但他們在部分早期行銷影像中確實使用了人工智慧。他們聲稱刪除照片是因為那些設計已不再販售，而非人工智慧技術本身的問題。

paragraphs.en: This trend has raised serious concerns about honesty in advertising. Lisa Barber, the editor of a tech group called Which?, noted that their research found a worrying 70% of people could not correctly tell the difference between real and fake videos. This means that many consumers are being misled without knowing it. Barber warned that this makes it much easier for scammers to trick people online. Because there are currently no specific rules requiring brands to tell customers when they are using AI, many people feel that the situation is becoming unfair.
paragraphs.zh: 此趨勢引發了對廣告誠信的嚴重擔憂。科技組織 Which? 的編輯芭芭拉 (Lisa Barber) 指出，他們的研究發現，高達百分之七十的人無法正確分辨真實影片與虛假影片的差異，這令人擔憂。這意味著許多消費者在不知情的情況下受到誤導。芭芭拉警告，這使得詐騙者更容易在網路上欺騙民眾。由於目前沒有明確規定要求品牌在運用人工智慧時必須告知顧客，許多人認為這種情況正變得不公平。

paragraphs.en: Different parts of the world are reacting to this problem in different ways. In the European Union, new rules under the Artificial Intelligence Act will start in August. These laws will require AI-generated content—such as deepfakes, which are realistic but fake videos or images—to be clearly labeled so that viewers know what they are seeing. However, these laws will not apply in the United Kingdom, leaving a gap in protection for consumers there. The group Which? has argued that customers should always be informed when they are looking at promotional content featuring AI-generated people instead of real individuals.
paragraphs.zh: 世界各地正以不同方式應對此問題。在歐盟，人工智慧法案 (Artificial Intelligence Act) 下的新規定將於八月生效。這些法律將要求人工智慧生成的內容（例如深偽技術，即逼真但虛假的影片或影像）必須清楚標示，以便觀眾了解自己所見為何。然而，這些法律並不適用於英國，導致當地消費者保護出現缺口。Which? 組織主張，當顧客看到的是由人工智慧生成的人物而非真人所呈現的促銷內容時，應該始終獲得告知。

paragraphs.en: As technology continues to improve, it will become even harder to spot the difference between a real person and a digital creation. For now, experts suggest that consumers should be more careful about the content they see on their screens. If an influencer’s story seems too perfect or if a photo looks slightly strange, it might be a sign that the person is not real. As companies continue to experiment with these tools, the debate over how much honesty is required in digital advertising is likely to grow. For the average user, the best advice is to stay informed and keep a healthy level of doubt about the advertisements that appear in their daily feed.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著技術持續進步，分辨真人與數位創作的差異將變得更加困難。目前，專家建議消費者應對螢幕上看到的內容更加謹慎。如果某位網紅的故事看起來太過完美，或者照片看起來有些怪異，這可能就是該人物並非真人的跡象。隨著企業持續嘗試這些工具，關於數位廣告需要多少誠信的辯論可能會愈演愈烈。對於一般使用者而言，最好的建議是保持資訊靈通，並對每日動態中出現的廣告保持適度的懷疑。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move through text or images on a computer or phone screen.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（卷軸），這裡作動詞，指在數位裝置上瀏覽內容。文中：When you scroll through your social media feed, you might see a person recommending a new app, a fashion brand, or a travel service.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To attract or hold someone's attention.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「捕捉」實體物品，這裡指吸引目光。文中：They described the practice as an experiment to see what kind of content catches the attention of their audience.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To notice or identify someone or something, especially when it is difficult to see.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（斑點），這裡作動詞，指在眾多事物中辨識出某物。文中：As technology continues to improve, it will become even harder to spot the difference between a real person and a digital creation.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Brands using AI-generated influencers to promote products on social media

quiz.question: According to the article, why did the brand Ashle remove photos of a woman from their social media?

options.A: They were pressured by The Guardian to admit the images were fake.
options.B: The clothing items featured in the images were no longer available for purchase.
options.C: They wanted to hide the fact that the woman in the photo had an extra finger.
options.D: They realized that using AI-generated influencers was against the law.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，Ashle 的發言人解釋刪除照片的原因是該服裝設計已不再販售，而非因為 AI 技術本身。選項 A 錯誤，雖然他們被聯繫後刪除照片，但並非因為被強迫承認造假。選項 C 雖提到了多出一根手指的錯誤，但這僅是網友發現的瑕疵，並非官方聲稱的刪除動機。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提到當時有相關法律限制其行為。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the future of online advertising?

options.A: Companies will likely stop using AI influencers due to public backlash.
options.B: It will become increasingly difficult for consumers to distinguish between authentic and artificial content.
options.C: The European Union's new laws will effectively eliminate all AI-generated scams globally.
options.D: Most companies will voluntarily disclose their use of AI to maintain customer trust.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第七段，文章指出隨著技術進步，分辨真實人類與數位創作將變得更加困難。選項 A 錯誤，文中提到廣告商持續實驗這些工具，並未顯示停止跡象。選項 C 錯誤，歐盟的法律僅適用於歐盟境內，無法解決全球性的詐騙問題。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到許多公司甚至簽署保密協議來隱藏 AI 的使用，顯示企業並非主動透明。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by a 'healthy level of doubt'?

options.A: A suspicious attitude that leads to constant stress.
options.B: A reasonable amount of skepticism when viewing online content.
options.C: A medical condition caused by staring at screens for too long.
options.D: A complete refusal to believe anything seen on social media.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「healthy level of doubt」意指在面對網路廣告時，應保持適度的懷疑與警覺，而非盲目相信。選項 A 錯誤，healthy 在此並非指造成壓力的負面情緒。選項 C 錯誤，這是對詞彙的錯誤解讀，與醫學無關。選項 D 錯誤，作者建議的是保持懷疑，而非完全否定所有資訊，這兩者程度不同。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: AI technology is the most effective way for companies to increase their sales.
options.B: The government should ban all AI-generated images to protect the public.
options.C: The rise of AI influencers creates challenges for consumer transparency and honesty in advertising.
options.D: Social media users are becoming better at identifying AI-generated content over time.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文核心在於討論 AI 網紅的興起如何導致消費者被誤導，以及缺乏透明度所引發的誠信問題。選項 A 錯誤，文章並未評估 AI 的銷售成效，而是聚焦於其帶來的負面影響。選項 B 錯誤，文中僅提到歐盟要求標示，並未主張全面禁止。選項 D 錯誤，文中明確指出 70% 的人無法分辨真偽，顯示大眾辨識能力並未提升。
